mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held flat or declining over the trailing 3 months signals accommodative monetary policy, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following flat/declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically higher than returns following rising FEDFUNDS (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window from cyclical lows, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that precedes broader earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% cyclical low as of 2020-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with historically low unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered UNRATE-rise instances, or the effect disappears when tested outside recessionary onset periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in INDPRO growth alongside stagnant or declining RSAFS growth indicates a joint supply-demand slowdown that is not captured by either series alone, and jointly signals equity risk deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 1%
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO/RSAFS slowdown condition triggers but 20-day forward SPX returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a sustained decline in HOUST (housing starts) over 6 months signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that historically leads broader economic deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is expected to be weak or absent in early-cycle recovery regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1608.0 declining toward revised 1548.0 as of 2019-12
failure_condition: HOUST decline signal produces no statistically significant negative skew in 60-day forward returns during late-cycle windows (t-test p > 0.05), or the sign flips positive in more than 2 of 4 historical late-cycle episodes tested.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS reflects strengthening labor demand and consumer income generation, providing a leading always-on positive signal for near-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as payroll surprises are quickly priced by equity markets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the relationship reverses in more than 40% of quarters tested.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 2.5%, it raises the probability of restrictive Fed policy response, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice rate-hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI threshold crossings are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across a rolling 5-year sample, or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong RSAFS growth and weak or negative GDPC1 growth signals unsustainable consumption financed by credit or drawdown rather than genuine output expansion, jointly predicting a mean-reverting negative S&P 500 forward return over the next 60 trading days as the divergence resolves.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 4% while GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2%
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 divergence condition triggers but 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the univariate RSAFS or GDPC1 effects alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a rebound in INDPRO of more than 1% month-over-month after a preceding contraction signals restarting industrial capacity and forward earnings recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; outside recovery regimes this signal carries no reliable predictive content.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change > 1% following at least 2 prior months of contraction
failure_condition: The INDPRO rebound signal fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during identified post-shock recovery periods, or produces equivalent hit rates outside recovery regimes, indicating no regime dependency.
